items.name: +1 Cold Iron Light Hammer
items.description: This smaller version of the warhammer has a wooden or metal shaft ending in a metal head. Unlike its heavier cousin, it is light enough to throw. Cold iron weapons deal additional damage to creatures with weakness to cold iron, like demons and fey.

items.name: +1 Striking Pick
items.description: A pick designed solely for combat has a sturdy wooden shaft and a heavy, pointed head to deliver devastating blows.

items.name: Anticorrosion Oil
items.description: You can coat objects of 6 Bulk or less with anticorrosion oil. For 24 hours, the object takes half damage from acid and from all effects that specifically cause it to rust or corrode, such as contact with a rust monster's antennae.

items.name: Backpack
items.description: A backpack for holding your gear

items.name: Bedroll
items.description: A compact mat, not too comfy but suitable for long journeys.

items.name: Chalk
items.description: Suitable for marking hard surfaces temporarily.

items.name: Crafter's Eyepiece
items.description: This rugged metal eyepiece etched with square patterns is designed to be worn over a single eye. Twisting the lens reveals a faint three-dimensional outline of an item you plan to build or repair, with helpful labels on the component parts. You gain a +1 item bonus to Crafting checks. When you Repair an item, increase the Hit Points restored to 10 + 10 per proficiency rank on a success or 15 + 15 per proficiency rank on a critical success.

items.name: Crescent Cross
items.description: A crescent cross combines a small scizore with an arm-mounted crossbow apparatus that can hold up to three bolts at a time. It changes chambers automatically after you fire. To reload, you must load each bolt into the aparatus individually as its own action.

items.name: Eye Garnets
items.description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

items.name: Flint & Steel
items.description: A camper's classic for starting fires.

items.name: Full Plate
items.description: Plate mail consists of interlocking plates that encase nearly the entire body in a carapace of steel. It is costly and heavy, and the wearer often requires help to don it correctly, but it provides some of the best defense armor can supply. A suit of this armor comes with an undercoat of padded armor and a pair of gauntlets.

items.name: Gecko Potion
items.description: A gecko potion is a sticky, tawny brown liquid with flecks of sand suspended in it. For 5 minutes after drinking this potion, your fingertips sprout thousands of microscopic, bristled hairs that cling to objects, granting you a +1 item bonus to Climb and Palm an Object, and to your Reflex DC against Disarm attempts.

items.name: Gourd Head
items.description: You have a large gourd for a skull, and since you don't have a physical brain, you can use the space inside your head. You can store a collection of up to 1 Bulk of objects within your head. The DC of checks to Steal objects from inside your head increases by 4. Additionally, if you store only one object within your head, you can draw it effortlessly into your hand as part of another action to use the object. Drawing the item grants this other action the manipulate trait.

items.name: Healer's Toolkit
items.description: This kit of bandages, herbs, and suturing tools is necessary for Medicine checks to Administer First Aid, Treat Disease, Treat Poison, or Treat Wounds. If you wear your healer's toolkit, you can draw and replace them as part of the action that uses them.

items.name: Horn of Rust
items.description: This rusted, coiled iron horn is sizable, appearing similar in shape to a ram's horn but measuring nearly 2 feet in diameter. Flakes of rust constantly fall from it, and the item leaves hands with a rusty red stain that lingers. While the Horn of Rust's power has diminished over the ages since Xar-Azmak's death, those who carry it are still subject to its palpable chaotic influences. If you are not sanctified Unholy, you are Enfeebled 1 while carrying or using the Horn of Rust; if you are sanctified Holy, you are instead Enfeebled 2 and Stupefied 1. It can be prepared as a staff, granting four spells.

actions.name: Blow the Horn
actions.description: Once per hour, you can blow on the horn to unleash a swirling aura of jagged, rusty metal shards that orbit you in a 5-foot emanation for 1 minute. The shards grant you a +1 item bonus to AC. Creatures and objects that begin their turn in this area take 6d6 slashing damage from the shards (DC 19 basic Reflex save). A creature damaged by this aura is exposed to a poten form of Tetanus that is a Level 5 disease and has a DC 19 Fortitude Save. Worshippers of Xar-Azmak are immune to this effect.

actions.name: Consume Corruption
actions.description: Once per day, you can place the horn's bell against a diseased creature and then inhale into the mouthpiece rather than blow into it. The horn attempts to draw out the disease, casting Cleanse Affliction with a counteract modifier of +11. If the Horn of Rust succeeds at countering the disease, it gains a number of staff charges equal to half the disease's level rounded down. If it critically fails, not only is the disease not countered, but you are exposed to the disease and must attempt a saving throw against it to resist contracting the affliction yourself.

items.name: Longbow
items.description: This 5-foot-tall bow, usually made of a single piece of elm, hickory, or yew, has a powerful draw and is excellent at propelling arrows with great force and at an extreme distance. You must use two hands to fire a longbow, and it can't be used while mounted. Because it's made for long range, you take a -2 penalty to attacks on creatures within 30ft with it.

items.name: Midnight Milk
items.description: The strange, swirling liquid in this bottle is said to give those who injest it a feeling of drowsy euphoria as it lulls them to sleep. It is a new drug, making its way into surface markets via trade routes in the Darklands.

items.name: Minor Elixir of Life
items.description: Elixirs of life accelerate a living creature's natural healing processes and immune system. Upon drinking this elixir, you regain 1d6 Hit Points and gain a +1 item bonus to saving throws against diseases and poisons for 10 minutes.

items.name: Minor Healing Potion
items.description: A healing potion is a vial of a ruby-red liquid that imparts a tingling sensation as the drinker's wounds heal rapidly. When you drink a minor healing potion, it restores 1d8 HP when consumed

items.name: Moderate Drakeheart Mutagen
items.description: Your skin grows scales like a drake, and your eyesight become sharp and your pupils slitted, but your mind and reflexes slow. You gain a +5 Item bonus to AC, a Dexterity cap of +2 (as usual, use your lowest Dexterity cap if you have more than one), and a +2 item bonus to Perception checks. If you're wearing armor, you still calculate your proficiency bonus to AC based on your proficiency in the armor you're wearing, even if the drakeheart mutagen has a higher item bonus. While under its effects, you take a –1 penalty to Will saves, Reflex saves, and all skill checks to Recall Knowledge. As a single action, you can burn through the remaining duration of the Mutagen to stride twice.

items.name: Mother's Amulet
items.description: A blood-red ruby set in irridescent metal, the last gift your mother gave you.

reactions.name: Amulet's Abeyance
reactions.trigger: The target of your Exploit Vulnerability would damage you or an ally within 15 feet of you and you are holding your implement.
reactions.description: You forcefully present your amulet to turn away harm. You or a target ally within 15 feet gain resistance to all damage against the triggering damage. The resistance is equal to 2 + your level.

items.name: Mysterious Blue Orb
items.description: Streaks of blue shimmer within the smoky depths of this perfect crystalline sphere. Both the scintillating shade and emanations of an indefinable power suggest that something slumbers within. When all is quiet, Blu can feel it pulling at him.

items.name: Oil of Weightlessness
items.description: You can spread this shimmering oil on an item of 1 Bulk or less to make it feel weightless. It has negligible Bulk for 1 hour.

items.name: Potion of Waterbreathing
items.description: This filmy, gray potion reeks of ocean detritus and tastes even worse. After drinking this potion, you gain the ability to breathe underwater for 1 hour.

items.name: Rations
items.description: While they don't taste amazing, rations offer enough nutrition to keep you from starving. Each pack can feed a single person for a day.

items.name: Reinforced Tower Shield
items.description: These massive shields can be used to provide cover to nearly the entire body. Due to their size, they are typically made of wood reinforced with metal. You provide cover for allies behind you when you raise a tower shield, and can increase the bonus to AC from +2 to +4 by Taking Cover after Raising the shield.

items.name: Rope (50ft)
items.description: Sturdy rope for tying something (or someone) down.

items.name: Rust Scrub
items.description: This salt scrub is infused with citron juice and alchemical ingredients that allow it to fight rust on metallic equipment. One application can be used to restore 2d4 Hit Points to an item that has been damaged by rust. The GM determines what portion of damage to an item was caused by rust if that value isn't known.

items.name: Rusty, Broken Full Plate Armor
items.description: Large red rubies you pried from a statue of Xar-Azmak.

items.name: Sanitizing Pin
items.description: This pin includes a piece of gauze wrapped around a dried herbal poultice. When you activate the sanitizing pin, the poultice dissolves and bolsters your body's defense against poison or disease. You gain a +2 status bonus on your Fortitude save against the initial exposure to the affliction. If you roll a critical failure on the triggering attempt, you get a failure instead.

items.name: Scroll of Cleanse Affliction
items.description: A single-use scroll

items.name: Scroll of Dispel Magic
items.description: Single-use scroll

items.name: Scroll of Friendfetch
items.description: A single-use scroll

items.name: Scroll of Pocket Library
items.description: Single-use scroll

items.name: Scroll of Runic Body
items.description: Single-use scroll

items.name: Scroll of Sleep
items.description: Single-use scroll

items.name: Shuriken
items.description: This “throwing star” is a small piece of flat metal with sharp edges, designed to be flung with a flick of the wrist.

items.name: Skeleton Key
items.description: A grinning skull tops the bow of this macabre key. This key can be used in place of a thieves' toolkit when attempting to Pick a Lock, and it grants a +1 item bonus to the Thievery check. If the skeleton key becomes broken due to a critical failure on the check, it works as a normal thieves' toolkit and loses its benefits until repaired.

freeActions.name: Loosen Lock
freeActions.description: Once per day, the key casts Knock on a Lock you're trying to pick, granting a +4 status bonus to attempts to pick it.

secret.name: Shrieking Key
secret.description: A skull-topped metal shrieking key appears to be a skeleton key, useful in place of thieves' tools when attempting to Pick a Lock. A shrieking key has no activation, however. When you use it to Pick a Lock, the key emits a loud shriek audible for 500 feet despite ambient noise. Physical barriers still block or muffle the shriek as normal. You also take a –2 circumstance penalty to the Thievery check rather than enjoying a bonus. After you attempt such a check with the key the first time, it magically attaches to you, returning to your possession if discarded. To use another device to Pick a Lock, you must first succeed at a DC 20 Will save.

items.name: Soap
items.description: Scrub a dub dub

items.name: Spacious Pouch
items.description: Though it appears to be a cloth bag decorated with panels of richly colored silk or stylish embroidery, a spacious pouch opens into a magical space larger than its outside dimensions. The Bulk held inside the bag doesn't change the Bulk of the spacious pouch itself. The amount of Bulk the bag's extradimensional space can hold depends on its type. You can Interact with the spacious pouch to stow items in it or remove them just like a mundane sack. Though the bag can hold a great amount of material, an object still needs to be able to fit through the opening of the sack to be stored inside. If the bag is overloaded or broken, it ruptures and is ruined, causing the items inside to be lost forever. If it's turned inside out, the items inside spill out unharmed, but the bag must be put right before it can be used again. A living creature placed inside the bag has enough air for 10 minutes before it begins to suffocate, and it can attempt to Escape against a DC of 13. An item inside the bag provides no benefits unless it's retrieved first. An item in the bag can't be detected by magic that detects only things on the same plane.

items.name: Spiked Steel Shield
items.description: Like wooden shields, steel shields come in a variety of shapes and sizes. Though more expensive than wooden shields, they are much more durable.

items.name: Sterling Artisan's Tools (Talismans)
items.description: An incredibly high-quality set of artisan tools, perfectly suited to creating Talismans. If you use them when you Craft a Talisman, you get a +1 item bonus to the check.

items.name: Studded Leather Armor
items.description: This leather armor is reinforced with metal studs and sometimes small metal plates, providing most of the flexibility of leather armor with more robust protection.

items.name: Thieves' Toolkit
items.description: You need a thieves' toolkit to Pick Locks or Disable Devices (of some types) using the Thievery skill. If your thieves' toolkit is broken, you can repair it by replacing the lock picks with replacement picks appropriate to your toolkit; this doesn't require using the Repair action. If you wear your thieves' toolkit, you can draw and replace it as part of the action that uses it.

items.name: Torch
items.description: A torch sheds bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) for 1 hour. It can be used as an improvised weapon that deals 1d4 bludgeoning damage plus 1 fire damage when lit.

items.name: Vocoder of Invisibility
items.description: A curious device found in a crashed spaceship in the deserts of Numeria.

actions.name: Unseen Din
actions.description: You engage a piece of the alien technology, producing a deafening sound as you become completely invisible. Because of the noise, you are only Concealed to other creatures unless they are deaf. This effect lasts until the end of your next turn.

items.name: Wand of Breathe Fire
items.description: A wand containing the spell Breathe Fire

items.name: Wand of Cleanse Affliction
items.description: A wand containing the spell Cleanse Affliction

items.name: Wand of Create Food
items.description: A wand containing the spell Create Food

items.name: Wand of Dizzying Colors
items.description: A wand containing the spell Dizzying Colors

items.name: Wand of Grease
items.description: A wand containing the spell Grease.

items.name: Waterskin
items.description: When it's full, a waterskin contains roughly 1 day's worth of water for a Small or Medium creature.

items.name: Wolf Fang
items.description: This wolf canine is bound in a strip of leather and tied to a buckle or strap on a suit of armor. When the fang is affixed to a weapon and you succesfully trip a foe, you can activate it to deal bludgeoning damage equal to your Strength modifier to the target of your Trip.

items.name: Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer
items.description: At the height of the ancient Thassilonian Empire Emperor Xin crafted seven Alara'quin, magical weapons of extraordinary power. Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer was the symbol of office for Xanderghul, the Runelord of Pride.

reactions.name: Dominant Emotion (Pride)
reactions.trigger: You would be adversely affected by an emotion effect
reactions.description: The hammer tries to neutralize the triggering effect with its own emotion, attempting a counteract check. If the check succeeds, you feel a sudden rush of intense pride and the counteracted effect doesn't take hold of you.

staff.name: Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer
staff.description: At the height of the ancient Thassilonian Empire Emperor Xin crafted seven Alara'quin, magical weapons of extraordinary power. Xanderghul's Flawless Hammer was the symbol of office for Xanderghul, the Runelord of Pride.
